Tehran's nuclear programme and other points in the agreement are conditional on a halt in fighting in Lebanon. items.description: Lebanon reported 18 deaths on 19 June after Israeli airstrikes hit several locations in the country's south and east, raising fresh concerns about the durability of a regional peace initiative announced earlier this week.

items.title: US curbs Anthropic AI access, raising global concerns
items.description: The Trump administration has restricted foreign access to Anthropic's latest AI models over security fears, forcing a global shutdown. Could the row hurt the Claude-maker's IPO plans and set a far-reaching precedent?

items.title: Backstage at Gorillaz' epic, one-off stadium show: 'The vibe is ridiculous'
items.description: Damon Albarn, De La Soul and Moonchild Sannelly talk backstage as Gorillaz play their biggest show.

items.title: Catholic Church urges Congress to reject Milei's property bill
items.description: Church bodies warn that Milei's government's 'Inviolability of Private Property' bill threatens national sovereignty over land, water and natural resources and call on lawmakers to vote it down. Leer más

items.title: Colombia election: Hard-right candidate claims victory
items.description: Right-wing presidential candidate, Abelardo de la Espriella, won 49.66% of the Colombians' votes, according to preliminary results.

items.title: BBC sees destroyed villages in Israeli-occupied southern Lebanon
items.description: Travelling with a humanitarian convoy, BBC's Hugo Bachega has been given rare access to a part of Lebanon under Israeli occupation.

items.title: Berlin summit brings out private equity protesters
items.description: Activists gathered to protest the SuperReturn private equity summit in Berlin, saying the industry drives inequality, job cuts and rising costs as investors representing trillions of Euros gather to seek high returns.

items.title: Thousands join International Yoga Day events worldwide
items.description: International Day of Yoga is celebrated globally on June 21 every year, since its adoption by the United Nations in 2014

items.title: Haitians hope for a victory against Brazil in the World Cup
items.description: Haitians are fervent soccer fans and deeply patriotic toward their nation — the world’s first Black-led republic and the second independent republic in the Americas after the U.S. But for decades, they’ve rooted for Brazil.

items.title: German journalist detained in Syria back home, lawyer says
items.description: Eva Maria Michelmann vanished in Syria in January as government forces advanced on Kurdish fighters in the northeast. The German government last week said it was working to get her home.

items.title: Malaysia’s 3rd International Summit of Religious Leaders explores how faith can empower the youth
items.description: Religious leaders, scholars and students gathered in Kuala Lumpur for the Third International Summit of Religious Leaders, focusing on empowering youth in an era of rapid technological and social change.

items.title: Zelensky stripped of Poland's top award amid diplomatic spat
items.description: Ukraine's president has returned a state decoration he received from Poland amid a diplomatic spat. Poland stripped Volodymyr Zelensky of the award after Zelensky renamed a ⁠military unit in honour of the World War Two-era Ukrainian Insurgent Army. The insurgents were involved in massacres which Poland considers a genocide. Two former Ukrainian presidents and other senior officials also returned awards received from Poland in protest. Our correspondent in Kiev, Emmanuelle Chaze, reports.

items.title: Argentina confirms awarding of concession for strategic Paraná-Paraguay waterway
items.description: Government confirms consortium of Belgian company Jan De Nul and local firm Servimagnus will be granted a 25-year concession to dredge and maintain Paraguay-Paraná waterway. Leer más

items.title: Niger: search underway for attackers who killed 11 soldiers at Niamey airport
items.description: Niger's security apparatus has begun a search for the attackers who attacked the airport of the capital, Niamey, on Thursday, killing at least 11 soldiers and two civilians.

items.title: New wave of anti-LGBTQ laws sweeps Africa
items.description: A string of west African countries have outlawed same-sex relations in recent months, further eroding LGBTQ rights on a continent where they were already under attack

items.title: Colombia runoff vote shaped by security fears and conflict warnings
items.description: Voters head to a runoff in Colombia Sunday between candidates offering sharply different approaches to armed groups, with the frontrunner calling for intensified military action over peace talks

items.title: Hormuz dispute clouds Iran peace talks
items.description: At the Lake Lucerne summit, U.S. and Iranian officials are trying to keep a shaky peace effort alive after renewed violence and confusion over Hormuz. Former Ambassador Ryan Crocker talks about the need for strategic patience.

items.title: Algeria file complaint over referee's decision on Messi tackle
items.description: Argentina's skipper caught Algeria defender Aissa Mandi on the calf with a studs-up challenge from behind in the 30th minute of Tuesday's match but was not even booked. Leer más

items.title: Ethiopia PM's party wins landslide as fears grow of new conflict
items.description: Abiy Ahmed's party retains its huge majority despite unrest in several parts of Ethiopia and tensions with its neighbours.

items.title: U.S. and Iran conclude high-level talks in Switzerland, mediators say
items.description: A joint statement from mediating nations ​Qatar and Pakistan said the U.S. and Iran agreed to a roadmap toward a final deal within 60 days

items.title: Ivory Coast police arrest man over murky Abidjan homes demolition
items.description: Ivory Coast police said Friday they had arrested a man accused of using a fake court document to order the demolition of a large area of an Abidjan neighbourhood.

items.title: African and Caribbean nations call for formal apology for transatlantic slavery
items.description: The leaders have asked for apologies from the countries that benefited from the slave trade, as well as debt relief and financial compensation.

items.title: Manuel Adorni asset declaration shows wealth grew 45-fold in 2024
items.description: Cabinet chief’s documentation attempts to explain an astronomical rise in his net worth. His legal strategy and mounting complications in Congress. Leer más

items.title: What Iran and US get from deal and why both could struggle to keep it
items.description: BBC analysts assess the claims by both Washington and Tehran to have won a victory with the deal to end their war.

items.title: UK's Starmer mulls 'political realities' after Burnham by-election
items.description: A member of UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer's Cabinet said it would be "delusional" to pretend there was no threat to his position. But he also said he had no information suggesting he might quit, as a newspaper reported.

items.title: Ángel Di María backs Argentina from afar: 'With you until the end of the world'
items.description: For the first time in 16 years, Ángel Di María will not lace up his boots at a World Cup, having appeared in four editions of the tournament. Leer más

items.title: Court warns former president Cristina Fernández de Kirchner over supporters' rallies outside home
items.description: Court warns former president over supporters' rallies outside her home and says she must comply with conditions of house arrest or face seeing restrictions tightened. Leer más

items.title: Milei’s Cabinet chief claims to be the crypto investor nobody knew
items.description: Argentina’s well-established crypto community is struggling to buy Manuel Adorni’s explanation to rebut allegations of illicit enrichment. Leer más

items.title: Unstoppable Messi hits another high with Selección’s solid support
items.description: Argentina's national team opened their World Cup campaign with a comfortable 3–0 win over Algeria, thanks to a hat trick from the extraterrestrial born in Rosario. Six World Cups, 16 World Cup goals for Messi – and counting... Leer más

items.title: Maradona’s former aide tells trial that star was manipulated
items.description: Support worker who aided Maradona in his final days says star was manipulated by those around him. Leer más

items.title: Morocco captain Achraf Hakimi to face rape trial, French court rules
items.description: A French appeals court on Friday confirmed that Moroccan football star Achraf Hakimi will face trial for the alleged rape of a woman in 2023, which the Paris Saint-Germain defender denies.

items.title: De la Espriella wins razor-thin majority in Colombia's election, Cepeda calls for final count
items.description: Conservative political outsider Abelardo de la Espriella won a razor-thin lead in Colombia's runoff presidential election on Sunday, a result that will be challenged in the coming days by the ruling party’s progressive candidate. Read our live blog to see how all the day's events unfolded.

items.title: South Africa ties Czech Republic 1-1, Mexico goes to knockout stage
items.description: South Africa keeps its World Cup hopes alive with a 1-1 draw against the Czech Republic, while Mexico reached the knockout stage after a 1-0 win against South Africa on Thursday.

items.title: Shipping stalls in Strait of Hormuz after Iran declares key waterway shut
items.description: Ship tracking data shows sharp fall in transits as US and Iranian officials hold talks to save fragile peace framework.

items.title: Ukrainian attacks prompt Russian-held Crimea to halt civilian gasoline sales
items.description: Governor Sergey Aksyonov, the Kremlin-appointed head of Crimea, said that overnight Ukrainian strikes killed four people and wounded 28 others. He did not specify the target of the attack.

items.title: Pressure mounts on UK Prime Minister Starmer amid reports he could resign within days
items.description: Embattled UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer was reflecting on the “political realities” facing his leadership, a senior minister said Sunday, as media reports suggested he could resign within days. The speculation follows rival Andy Burnham’s decisive by-election victory, which has strengthened expectations that the Labour heavyweight will challenge Starmer for the party leadership and premiership.

items.title: Africa must invest own funds in Ebola response, says CDC head
items.description: Africa's Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) chief said Friday the continent needs to invest its own funds in Ebola response.

items.title: Japanese pop group XG went from brutal five year training to global pop stars
items.description: The Japanese pop group have become global stars, after being recruited before they were teenagers.

items.title: Toyota eyes lunar rover powered by regenerative fuel-cell tech
items.description: Toyota has teamed up with JAXA since 2019 to develop the manned lunar rover — which it dubbed the Lunar Cruiser — that they hope can be put on the moon in 2029.

items.title: Lula jokes he is thinking of 'signing Messi' for Brazil
items.description: Brazil president jokes that his nation should hire Argentina's Lionel Messi to play for his nation's football team. Leer más

items.title: Dallas artist unveils mural paying tribute to Argentina and Lionel Messi
items.description: José May, known as 'Joe Skilz' spent a week in heat and rain painting the picture on a wall in the city's Deep Ellum district.

items.title: Why an AI company cleaned my New York City apartment for free
items.description: An AI company is sending free cleaners door-to-door in a bid to train the robots it hopes one day will replace them.

items.title: Why are European nations now moving to recognise Palestine? | Explained
items.description: Can the European Union coordinate a unified response to Palestine?

items.title: Adrián Ravier named Milei’s new presidential spokesperson
items.description: Forty-eight year-old accountant and economist and close presidential ally named to one of the most high-profile positions within Javier Milei’s government. Leer más

items.title: ‘Encouraging progress’ made as first round US-Iran talks end
items.description: The first round of US-Iran talks has ended with both sides agreeing on a roadmap towards a final deal.

items.title: Oil 'pouring through' Strait of Hormuz, Trump says, as he unveils new Air Force One
items.description: US president Donald Trump unveiled the new Airforce One, a jumbo jet formerly owned by Qatar. It features a navy blue and red exterior, a break from the traditional robin’s egg blue.

items.title: Celebrations as Mexico becomes first nation to reach World Cup knockout stage
items.description: Thousands of jubilant Mexico supporters celebrated across the country on 18 June after their team became the first nation to secure a place in the World Cup knockout stage with a 1-0 victory over South Korea.

items.title: Lula says Trump 'should not meddle' in Brazil's upcoming elections
items.description: Brazilian president says it's fine for Donald Trump to support the right-wing Bolsonaro family, but that he "should not meddle" in the nation's October presidential election. Leer más

items.title: World Bank issues Argentina guarantees for US$2-billion commercial loan
items.description: World Bank board approves guarantees for a commercial loan of as much as US$2 billion that Argentina intends to use to help pay down upcoming debt maturities. Leer más

items.title: Presidential pilots say they flew suitcases to Santa Cruz during Kirchner years
items.description: 'Cuadernos' graft trial hears from pilots, who allege presidential planes were used for delivery of suitcases and materials. Leer más

items.title: In Imo State, Governor Uzodinma seeks a chief judge he can own, By Chidi Anselm Odinkalu
items.description: The Acting Chief Judge whom Governor Uzodinma wishes to preclude from consideration does not have an adverse disciplinary record with the NJC. In this situation, the current Chief Justice of Nigeria has an opportunity to define the applicable conventions. It is not her job to grant the governor a Chief Judge whom he can own. […] The post In Imo State, Governor Uzodinma seeks a chief judge he can own, By Chidi Anselm Odinkalu appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Russia, Madagascar foreign ministers hold talks in Moscow
items.description: Russia's Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov welcomed his Madagascar counterpart, Alice N'Diaye, in Moscow on Friday
